serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of lion's mane mushroom thick broth rice is approximately 350 g, comprising a bowl of mixed-grain rice (white, red, brown rice and quinoa) topped with lion's mane mushroom in a thick savoury broth.

nutrient_source.title: Mixed grain rice with mushroom broth — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Cooked brown rice (FDC ID 168878), Cooked white rice (FDC ID 168878 analogue), Quinoa cooked (FDC ID 168917), Mushrooms Hericium erinaceus (FDC ID 169403), and HPB Singapore Foodo mixed grain rice reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of the declared ingredients: approximately 55% mixed grain rice (white/red/brown blend), 10% quinoa, 15% lion's mane mushroom, and 20% thick broth. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used for each component. HPB Singapore Foodo mixed grain rice data used as a cross-reference anchor. Values represent a plausible per-100g estimate for the combined dish as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed grain rice and quinoa blend — analogous estimate based on: 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021' (Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition) and University of Sydney GI Database entries for brown rice (GI ~55), white rice (GI ~72), quinoa (GI ~53)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. GI estimated as weighted average of components: white rice GI ~72 (University of Sydney GI Database), brown rice GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database), quinoa GI ~53 (Atkinson et al. 2021, AJCN). The mixed grain blend with broth dilution and lion's mane mushroom fibre content is expected to moderate the overall GI. Composite estimate of GI 55 is conservative and defensible. GL per serving = (55 × 22.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 43.3 g per 350 g serving.

hypertension.guidance: Each 350 g serving provides approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Mixed grain carbohydrate load with moderate glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: The mixed grain base (red rice, brown rice, quinoa) moderates the glycemic response compared to plain white rice (estimated GI 55), but the 350 g serving still delivers approximately 78.8 g of carbohydrate, requiring careful portion control and carbohydrate counting for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, whole grain components, and lion's mane mushroom beta-glucans
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (approximately 1.1 g per serving) and contains whole grain components and lion's mane mushroom, which provides beta-glucan dietary fibre associated with modest LDL cholesterol reduction; sodium from the broth should be monitored in individuals with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lion's Mane Mushroom Thick Broth Rice is a contemporary Singaporean café-style grain bowl served at Flavours by Saute (Funan, Singapore). The dish features a base of mixed grains — white rice, red rice, brown rice, and quinoa — enriched for higher protein and dietary fibre content compared to plain white rice. The grain blend is served with lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), a culinary and medicinal fungus prized in East Asian cuisine for its seafood-like texture and umami flavour, and ladled with a thick savoury broth. The overall texture is soft and hearty, with the thick broth providing a rich, slightly gelatinous mouthfeel. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~413 kcal per 350 g serving), meaningful protein (~13.3 g) from the quinoa and mushroom components, and dietary fibre (~6.3 g) that supports glycaemic moderation. The mixed grain base lowers the overall glycaemic index relative to plain white rice. Sodium content from the broth is the primary clinical consideration for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is suitable for vegetarian and vegan diets and is free of animal-derived ingredients. It is priced at SGD 14.30 and represents a health-oriented grain bowl positioned within the global wellness dining segment in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for the declared ingredients (mixed grain rice, quinoa, lion's mane mushroom, broth). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for the component grains. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on a direct laboratory analysis of this dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates; no direct laboratory analysis of this dish was available from approved sources. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources for this dish combination. 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as mushroom vitamin D content is highly variable depending on UV exposure and is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy entries used. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 5. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. 6. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg) is 0.0; confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients — naturally zero. 7. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed zero as no hydrogenated fats are present in the declared ingredients — naturally zero. 8. Cholesterol (cholesterol_mg) is 0.0; confirmed zero as this is a fully plant-based dish — naturally zero. 9. GI estimated at 55 (low category per ≤55 threshold) based on weighted average of component grains from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN. 10. Dietary flags reflect the declared ingredient list; halal status is assumed based on plant-based composition but has not been independently verified for the restaurant preparation. 11. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant bowl; actual portion may vary.
